Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The title and text explicitly refer to Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper repeatedly refers to SA-deficient sid2-2 plants and compares them with Col-0.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments were performed in Arabidopsis plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the experimental plant species throughout the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 and sid2-2 are explicitly used in Results figures and text.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No chloroplast localization for SID2 is stated in this paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: sid2-2 is explicitly described as SA-deficient / a salicylic acid biosynthesis mutant, linking SID2 to salicylic acid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper does not classify salicylic acid as a phenolic phytohormone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results and Discussion associate SA with plant defence and with antimicrobial/antibiofilm effects in leaf intercellular spaces.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: SA is measured in intercellular washing fluids and discussed as accumulating in leaf intercellular spaces/apoplast.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show sid2-2 partially restores multiplication and aggregate formation of Pst ΔalgD ΔalgU ΔmucAB and that PTI suppression is weaker in sid2-2 than Col-0.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper uses sid2-2 plants, in planta bacterial growth assays, fluorescence microscopy aggregate quantification, and SA biosensor assays in IWFs.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are directly reflected in Results sections 2.3, 2.5, and 2.7.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: sid2-2 is examined under Pst inoculation and under flg22 pretreatment to induce PTI.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show SA-deficient sid2-2 permits greater bacterial multiplication/aggregation, indicating compromised defence and increased pathogen success.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication from the findings, but the paper does not explicitly discuss breeding or engineering applications.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed focuses on SA-mediated defence and bacterial aggregate suppression rather than plant nutrient metabolism; this characterization matches the study context.
